reviews.review_msg: He has good ratings but I didn't like him personally. The midterms were fine they were reflective of the practice exams but the final was extremely hard and long which brought my grade down a lot. He was also absent a lot and wasn't even here the day of the final.
He was often absent, hard to reach, no practice final exam, no prep.
Didn't feel like he was very present as a prof this semester..

Very straightforward with exams and assignments, I recommend taking this with him. Very long lecture slides but they do provide lots of information. Tutorials were helpful to solve more practice problems and he also provided practice exams which were amazing. The assignments were somehow more difficult than the midterms but just prepare well!
Max teaches the concepts and the tutorial sections go over the quantitative questions. He is nice and very open to questions. 201 is great with him.
Do not take his course unless you want to teach yourself everything. His lectures are as if he is seeing the material for the first time. He often contradicts himself and seems confused.

Max is a nice guy, he's willing to answer questions. However, the content that he teaches in lecture is not very helpful for assignments and tests. His lectures are devoted to teaching concepts and theory however when it comes to solving physics, he rarely does it in class.

BRO JUST take it with him! he does not assume you know everything but rather he teaches you everything whether u know it or not. AND PLEASE GO TO HIS OFFICE HOURS HE IS AWSOME and doesn't judge even if u have no idea what anything is ( AKA MOMENTS) :) the best prof fr fr

I love Prof max he is hands down one of my fav profs this sem. Super nice and caring and always ready to answer all of your questions and if you need help he is more than happy to give you all the help you need. Exams are super fair and everything he taught in lec. 10/10 take this class with him if u have a chance u will not regret it
